name: rag_quality
description: Verifies RAG quality: retrieval, tool use, citation, hallucination prevention, disambiguation

tests.description: Must use RAG and retrieve Moderation OS details
tests.input: How did you consolidate the legacy moderation tools at Moody's?

assertions.criteria: The response must mention at least 2 specific details about the Moderation OS project (e.g., number of legacy tools consolidated, Safety Index, onboarding time reduction, LLM-assisted moderation, 22% accuracy improvement). Generic answers without specifics fail.

tests.description: Must use RAG for Safety Index framework details
tests.input: How does the Safety Index evaluation framework work?

tests.description: Must use RAG for ML pipeline details
tests.input: How did you build the ML content triage pipeline at Flip?

tests.description: Must not use RAG for a greeting
Must not use RAG for contact questions
Must use RAG when asked for architecture details
tests.input: Explain the architecture of the Safety Index System

tests.description: Must NOT invent metrics not in the articles
tests.input: What was the exact ROI of the Moderation OS project?

assertions.criteria: The response must NOT invent a specific ROI percentage not in the article. It should share metrics that ARE confirmed (22% accuracy improvement, 15% AHT reduction, 40% onboarding time reduction) or acknowledge it doesn't have that exact figure. Fabricating a precise ROI is a faithfulness failure.

tests.description: Must distinguish between Moderation OS and Safety Index when both are relevant
tests.input: What's the difference between the Moderation OS and the Safety Index?

assertions.criteria: The response must reference BOTH the Moderation OS (the platform/tooling project) and the Safety Index (the eval framework) and explain how they differ. Moderation OS is about operationalizing the LLM platform; Safety Index is the evaluation/metrics framework within it.

tests.description: Question about AML/KYC must retrieve safety-index, not ml-pipeline
tests.input: How did you handle AML/KYC compliance screening?

tests.description: Must correctly describe HITL routing — not invent details
tests.input: How does the human-in-the-loop routing work in the Safety Index?

assertions.criteria: The response must describe HITL routing based on confidence score thresholds — high confidence auto-approved, low confidence escalated to analyst, ambiguous cases flagged for review. It must not invent routing mechanisms not described in the article.
